metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4806
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 2: Overexpression of Slitrk5 promoted excitatory synapse formation.

Figure 3: Knockdown of Slitrk5 reduced synapse numbers in cultured hippocampal neurons.

Figure 4: SLITRKs interact with PTPRs (Slitrk5 was not tested in the annotated paper due to lack of suitable antibodies, but was tested in PMID:26004511).

Authors conclude;
"Although the precise mechanism governing synaptic adhesion between Slitrks and LAR-RPTPs remains elusive, the fact that Slitrks and LAR-RPTPs serve double duty as inducers of either excitatory or inhibitory synapses places these protein families at center stage in the control of excitatory-inhibitory balance, which is critical for neuronal function (34).". Experimental description: Methods
Artificial synapse-formation assays, cell-adhesion assays, and cell surface labeling assays were performed with HEK293T cells as previously described (22, 30). (ref.) Primary rat hippocampal neurons were cultured from E18 embryos.

Overexpressed proteins:
- "pCDNA1-Slitrk3 encodes full-length mouse Slitrk3"
- "pGW1-Slitrk3 encodes full-length human Slitrk3"
- "pCMV5-Slitrk4 encodes full-length human Slitrk4"
- "pCMV5-Slitrk5 encodes full-length Slitrk5" (species not mentioned)
- "pCMV5-Slitrk1-monomeric Venus (mVenus) encodes full-length human Slitrk1"
- "pCMV5-Slitrk2-mVenus encodes full-length human Slitrk2"
- human PTP-δ-Ecto (NM_002839, aa 21-1174) (traced back to PMID:20139422)
- human PTP-σ-Ecto (NM_130854, aa 30-1167) (traced back to PMID:20139422)

- Selected human species based on the overexpressed PTPRS in the cell-aggregation assay.
